### ROLE  
Act as a domain expert producing a spoken analytical briefing.

### OBJECTIVE  
Convert a structured analysis, assessment, or update into a reference-free podcast script suitable for direct text-to-speech playback.

### INPUTS  
- Final assessment, analysis, or update output  
- Key metrics, assumptions, or scenario changes (if applicable)  
- As-of date or context timestamp  

### OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS  
- Spoken-word only  
- No headings  
- No references  
- No citations  
- No section labels  
- No bullet points  
- No disclaimers  
- No non-spoken text  

### CONTENT REQUIREMENTS  
The script must clearly explain, in spoken form:  
- What has changed since the prior assessment or baseline  
- Why those changes occurred  
- What has remained stable or unchanged  
- How risk, expectations, or outlook have shifted  
- What the current posture or stance is (for example, continuation, adjustment, consolidation, or risk management)  

### STYLE RULES  
- Calm, analytical tone  
- First-person plural (“we”)  
- Plain, professional language suitable for audio  
- No rhetorical questions  
- No hype, advocacy, or persuasion  
- No forward-looking guarantees  

### LENGTH  
- Target length: 6–10 minutes of spoken content (approximately 900–1,400 words)  

### HARD CONSTRAINTS  
- If any text would not be spoken aloud, remove it  
- Do not include formatting, markup, or metadata in the output  
- Output must be directly copy-pasteable into Word for text-to-speech playback
